I recently completed my Certificate III in Individual Support (Ageing & Disability), including 120 hours of placement at Killara Glades Care Community in Sydney, NSW. During this time, I gained hands-on experience in supporting residents with personal care, daily activities, and emotional wellbeing. This experience helped me build confidence, patience, and a strong sense of responsibility in aged care.

After completing my placement in NSW, I relocated to South Australia and have been actively applying for aged care positions for the past three months. However, I have found it difficult to secure a role, as many employers require at least six months of experience. As someone who is ready to work and learn, this situation feels challenging, because without being given a chance, it is not possible to gain that experience.

I am genuinely passionate about working in aged care and making a positive difference in people’s lives. I am hardworking, compassionate, and willing to start in any role—casual, part-time, or even volunteer—to prove my abilities and gain further experience.

I kindly request you to consider my application and give me an opportunity to begin my career in aged care. I am confident that with the right support and guidance, I can contribute positively to your team and provide quality care to residents.

साच्चिकै एउटा कुरा शेयर गर्न मन लाग्यो, अनि सबैको opinion पनि चाहियो।

मेरो घरमा एउटा tenant बस्छ। सुरुमा म केही बोलिनँ, किनकि म आफैँ पनि विदेशमा पहिलोपटक एक्लै बस्दा कस्तो हुन्छ भन्ने बुझ्छु। त्यसैले सकेसम्म adjust गर्न खोजें।

तर बिस्तारै मैले notice गर्न थालेँ—धेरै पटक फ्रिजबाट मेरो groceries र खाना बिना सोधी प्रयोग भएको। सुरुमा लागेको थियो सायद mistake होला, तर यो बारम्बार हुन थाल्यो।

त्यसपछि अर्को कुरा—राति १२–१ बजे खाना पकाउने, कहिलेकाहीँ बिहान ६ बजे नै उठेर cooking गर्ने। त्यो पनि grinding/mashing machine र pressure cooker जस्तो आवाज आउने सामान प्रयोग गरेर। छिमेकीहरूले दुई पटक complaint गरिसके, landlord बाट warning समेत आइसकेको छ।

Kitchen को हालत त झन् अर्को कहानी छ—पकाएपछि भाँडा sink मा छोड्ने, सफा नगर्ने, gas र basin सबै अस्तव्यस्त। message गरेर सफा गर्न भनियो भने, धुने सट्टा भाँडा, cooker नै फ्रिजभित्र हालिने!

Bin कहिल्यै फालिएको छैन—recycling र normal waste मिसाएर हाल्ने, ठूला box हरू हालेर bin तुरुन्त भरिने।  
अनि toilet paper, cleaning सामान, bin bags—यी basic कुरामा पनि कुनै contribution छैन।

तर मैले के गरेँ भने—केही नभनी adjust गर्दै गएँ।

Nepali citizens are still required to travel to Canberra for passport renewal, even though consulates exist in other states. This is costly, time-consuming, and unfair—especially for students and workers.

📌 Passport services are a basic right.
We urgently call for:
Decentralised passport renewal at state consulates
Reliable online processing
Regular and accessible mobile services

Department of Passports Nepal
Ministry of Foreign Affairs Nepal
Embassy of Nepal in Canberra

Job Title: Home Support Worker for Autistic Child

Location: Morphett Vale SA 5162 

Position Type: Casual (Weekly15-24 hrs) 

Hourly Rate: $57.23 (ABN flats rate)

Job Description:
We are seeking a compassionate and dedicated Home Support Worker to provide personalized care and assistance to a 6-year-old girl with autism in her home environment. The ideal candidate will be nurturing, patient, and skilled in working with children with special needs, ensuring a supportive and safe environment tailored to her unique needs.

Key Responsibilities:

- Provide 1:1 support to the child, engaging her in activities that promote development and well-being.
- Assist with daily living skills, including preparing food and cleaning dishes.
- Create a structured and supportive routine to encourage the child’s growth and independence.
- Maintain a clean and organized living space to ensure a safe environment.
- Collaborate with parents or guardians to address the child's specific needs and progress.
- Ensure the emotional and physical well-being of the child at all times.

Qualifications:

- Minimum Certificate III in Disability or equivalent qualification.
- Experience working with children, particularly with autism or other developmental disorders, is highly desirable.
- Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation.
- Current police clearance and working with vulnerable people & children checks.
- Strong communication skills and the ability to connect with children.

Working Hours:
This position primarily requires morning and evening support. Flexibility in scheduling is essential.

Application Process:
Interested candidates are encouraged to apply by submitting their resume and a cover letter detailing their relevant experience and qualifications through bhndrsbn@gmail.com  or  0466069531 text me. Please note that this position is open to female applicants only. Normally we need 2/3 hours morning & 2/3 hours evening.

Join us in making a positive impact in the life of a young child while working within a supportive home environment!
